mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.18% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 9.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 9.5% exceeds trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when UNRATE > 9.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline by more than 5% month-over-month, signaling demand destruction that reduces near-term corporate revenue growth. The interaction of both demand indicators amplifies the negative signal relative to either series alone, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 360156M and HOUST at 593K require baseline comparison for decline detection
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 92.0 index points, indicating supply-side weakness that constrains output growth and signals stagflationary pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 92.5354 is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of instances when INDPRO < 92.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 net new jobs per month while unemployment remains elevated above 9.0%, jointly signaling labor market stagnation. The interaction of weak job creation and high unemployment amplifies recession risk, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50K and UNRATE > 9.0% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 130470K and UNRATE at 9.5% require MoM change calculation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows contraction or near-zero growth below 13,200 billion chained dollars, signaling economic recession that reduces equity valuations. This always-on mechanism produces negative 20-day returns whenever GDP is in contraction regime.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 13238.6B is above recession threshold but requires QoQ growth rate
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of quarters when GDPC1 shows contraction, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and forward SPX returns is < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 220 index points while federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating negative real rates that erode bond valuations and redirect capital away from equities. This regime-conditional mechanism produces negative 20-day returns in high-inflation, low-rate environments.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 217.224 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18% approach trigger conditions
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between CPI and FEDFUNDS is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rises above 93.5 index points while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 130,000 net new jobs monthly, jointly signaling robust supply expansion and labor market strength. This interaction-driven mechanism produces positive 5-day returns as markets reprice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 93.5 and PAYEMS monthly change > 130K simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 92.5354 and PAYEMS at 130470K require MoM change verification
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in > 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus additive single-series models.
